digest: Today's biggest story is Anthropic's major model releases — Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 are now available, though details remain sparse. The company is also facing significant political headwinds with Pentagon blacklisting concerns and ongoing battles with the Trump administration. Meanwhile, Claude Code continues its steady development with v2.1.74 bringing memory optimizations and context management improvements.

stories.headline: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6
stories.summary: Anthropic has announced the release of Claude Opus 4.6 [1] and Claude Sonnet 4.6 [2], marking the latest iterations of their flagship AI models. The announcements appear to be placeholder pages with minimal details currently available.

stories.headline: Anthropic Launches The Anthropic Institute
stories.summary: Anthropic has announced the creation of The Anthropic Institute [1], though specific details about the organization's mission and activities are not yet available from the announcement.

stories.headline: Legal Experts Say Anthropic Has Strong Case Against Pentagon Blacklisting
stories.summary: Legal experts believe Anthropic has a strong legal position in challenging potential Pentagon blacklisting measures [1]. The dispute has generated significant discussion, with some viewing the confrontation as necessary and timely [2][3].
key_points: Legal analysts see merit in Anthropic's defense against government restrictions
The case highlights broader tensions between AI companies and federal agencies

sources.title: Anthropic has strong case against Pentagon blacklisting, legal experts say
I'm glad the Anthropic fight is happening now
Anthropic vs. Trump Administration: What Happens When Firms Push Back

stories.perspectives: While legal experts are optimistic about Anthropic's case, there's broader debate about whether confronting the administration now is strategically wise versus potentially escalating tensions.

stories.headline: Claude Code v2.1.74 Improves Memory Management and Context Tools
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.74 introduces actionable suggestions for the `/context` command to help developers optimize memory usage and manage context-heavy tools [1]. The update also adds a configurable `autoMemoryDirectory` setting and fixes a critical memory leak in streaming API responses that caused unbounded memory growth in Node.js environments [1].
key_points: New `/context` command provides specific optimization tips for memory and capacity issues
Custom directory configuration available for auto-memory storage
Critical memory leak fix prevents unbounded RSS growth in Node.js

stories.headline: Claude Code v2.1.73 Adds Model Override Support and Enterprise Features
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.73 introduces `modelOverrides` settings that allow developers to map model picker entries to custom provider model IDs, including Bedrock inference profile ARNs [1]. The update also improves enterprise compatibility with better SSL certificate error guidance for corporate proxy environments [1].
key_points: Custom model ID mapping supports enterprise deployment patterns
Enhanced SSL certificate error handling for corporate environments
Fixes for permission prompt freezes and Git-related deadlocks

stories.headline: Developer Creates Context-Aware Permission Guard for Claude Code
stories.summary: A community developer has built 'nah', a context-aware permission guard specifically designed for Claude Code [1]. The project has gained attention on Hacker News, suggesting growing interest in security and permission management tools for AI coding assistants.
key_points: Open-source tool addresses permission management concerns
Shows community focus on security in AI-assisted development

sources.title: Show HN: A context-aware permission guard for Claude Code

stories.headline: Use @-mentions to give Claude Code precise file context
stories.summary: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, recommends using @-mentions to point Claude at specific files instead of letting it search. This dramatically reduces token usage and improves accuracy since Claude works with the exact files you intend rather than guessing from project structure.
actionable_steps: Type @ in Claude Code to see a file picker with fuzzy search
Select the specific files relevant to your task before writing your prompt
Combine multiple @-mentions for cross-file tasks like refactoring

stories.headline: Write a CLAUDE.md file to set project conventions once
stories.summary: Adding a CLAUDE.md to your project root lets you define coding standards, test commands, and architectural decisions that persist across every Claude Code session. Think of it as onboarding documentation that Claude reads automatically — you teach it once instead of repeating yourself.
actionable_steps: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root with build/test/lint commands
Add coding conventions like naming patterns, preferred libraries, and file structure
Include architectural decisions and constraints Claude should respect
Use /init in Claude Code to auto-generate a starter CLAUDE.md

stories.headline: Use Claude's extended thinking for complex architectural decisions
stories.summary: When asking Claude to make non-trivial design choices — like choosing between patterns, structuring a new module, or planning a migration — enabling extended thinking produces significantly better results. Claude will reason through trade-offs before committing to an approach, catching issues that quick responses miss.
actionable_steps: For architecture questions, ask Claude to 'think through the trade-offs' explicitly
Use Claude Code's plan mode (shift+tab) for multi-step tasks that need careful reasoning
Review Claude's reasoning in extended thinking to catch flawed assumptions early

sources.title: Tips for getting better architectural advice from Claude
